title: GPT-3 175B Model Training
description:
GPT-3 (Generative Pre-trained Transformer 3) is a 175 billion parameter language model trained by OpenAI.

Using a comprehensive life-cycle assessment methodology (as detailed by Patterson et al., 2025), researchers evaluated the training process from hardware maintenance through operational deployment, resulting in 50.5 tonnes of CO2e total emissions comprising embodied emissions from hardware manufacturing (11.2 tonnes, 22.2%), dynamic power consumption (24.69 tonnes, 48.9%), and idle infrastructure consumption (14.6 tonnes, 28.9%). Training consumed 433,196 kWh of electricity over 1,082,990 GPU hours using Nvidia A100 80GB GPUs on the Jean Zay cluster in France (57 gCO2e/kWh). Hardware embodied emissions were calculated by amortizing manufacturing footprint over 6-year equipment lifecycle at 85% utilization: 136,209 server-hours (48 nodes × 2,837.68 training hours) at 0.056 kg CO2e/h per server, plus 1,082,990 GPU-hours at 0.003 kg CO2e/h per GPU.

consumer.name: Infrastructure overhead
consumer.description: Total facility energy consumption with a PUE of 1.1 (Datacenter cooling,ligthing and other overhead systems)
